key_highlights: Geopolitical escalation: US-Iran strikes disrupt fragile ceasefire, spiking oil prices and dampening risk appetite.
AI and tech mega-cap rally continues: IREN, SK Hynix, Micron, and nuclear/space stocks surge on AI demand and IPO euphoria.
Policy shifts: Ohio halts data center tax credits; Illinois passes historic AI safety bill; Trump pledges crypto/prediction market support.
Macro crosscurrents: Tariff refunds ($20B paid), rising gas prices pressuring discretionary stocks, and yen intervention risk near 160/$.
Corporate governance turmoil: BP chair ousted; Starbucks 'Tank Day' backlash; Synopsys settles with Elliott.

sectors.trend: Strong bullish momentum driven by AI infrastructure demand, with IREN up 58% YTD, SK Hynix and Micron hitting $1T valuations, and Nvidia anticipation ahead of June 2 event.
sectors.implications: AI capex cycle remains intact; hyperscaler deals (Applied Digital) and nuclear/energy plays (Oklo, TeraWulf) benefit. Valuations are stretched but fundamentals support.

sectors.trend: Oil spikes on renewed Gulf attacks (headlines 6, 36, 45, 92), but peace talks cause whipsaw. IEA warns oil investments to drop for third year. US cooking gas cargoes canceled due to freight costs.
sectors.implications: Short-term volatility high; energy stocks may rally on supply disruption but long-term investment decline suggests structural shift. Natural gas weak on below-normal US temps.

sectors.trend: Space stocks (Rocket Lab, AST, Virgin Galactic) surge on SpaceX IPO euphoria and Starship explosion creating competitor opportunity. Nuclear stocks jump on Oklo's DOE plutonium talks.
sectors.implications: Space sector entering speculative phase; SpaceX IPO is a major catalyst. Defense spending may rise due to geopolitical tensions.

sectors.trend: Gas prices stalling discretionary stocks; Walmart (safe-haven) hit by high gas prices. AutoZone pullback seen as buying opportunity. Nike rebound unlikely. PacSun targets Gen Z.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending under pressure from energy costs; defensive positioning in staples may outperform. Luxury and big-ticket items face headwinds.

sectors.trend: Gold slumps to two-month low ($4500.40) as peace hopes fade; industrial metals decline on Gulf strikes dampening deal optimism. Sugar retreats with crude. Chinese coal disaster impacts steel/power.
sectors.implications: Gold remains a geopolitical hedge but near-term weakness. Base metals vulnerable to demand slowdown from China and trade uncertainty. Coal supply shock may boost prices.

sectors.trend: Trump pledges to protect crypto and prediction markets; Strive Asset Management lifts Bitcoin holdings to 16,500 tokens. Polymarket insider trading case (Google engineer) highlights regulatory scrutiny.
sectors.implications: Crypto-friendly regulatory environment under Trump could boost institutional adoption. Prediction markets face both opportunity and fraud risks.

sectors.trend: Treasuries decline on oil-driven inflation fears; yen intervention risk near 160/$. BOK hawkish hold; Nomura sees BOJ hike uncertain due to Iran. EM assets outperform (UBS).
sectors.implications: Bond yields may rise further if oil stays elevated. Yen weakness could trigger BOJ intervention. EM debt attractive on carry but vulnerable to risk-off.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk premium repricing (Iran strikes, Middle East instability)
AI infrastructure buildout driving tech and energy demand
Regulatory divergence: AI safety (Illinois) vs. crypto/prediction market support (Trump)
Consumer strain from rising gas prices and inflation
Space and nuclear renaissance as alternative AI power sources
China slowdown and commodity demand concerns

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase energy and defense exposure; hedge with gold and VIX; reduce EM and cyclical risk.
Favor TIPS, short-duration bonds, and commodity-linked equities; avoid rate-sensitive sectors.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory crackdown on AI and data centers
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across AI beneficiaries; monitor Ohio/Illinois policy spillover; favor companies with compliance infrastructure.
Rotate from discretionary to staples and discount retailers; monitor gas price impact on spending data.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: China economic weakness and commodity demand
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to base metals and China-sensitive equities; favor domestic demand stories.
Avoid companies with governance red flags (BP, Starbucks); favor firms with strong ESG/board oversight.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to AI infrastructure plays (IREN, Applied Digital, TeraWulf) ahead of Nvidia June 2 event.
opportunities.rationale: AI demand remains insatiable; hyperscaler deals and nuclear partnerships provide long-term revenue visibility.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy the dip in AutoZone and select consumer staples on gas price fears.
opportunities.rationale: AutoZone's pullback offers entry at reasonable valuation; staples like Walmart may rebound as gas prices stabilize.

opportunities.recommendation: Add to gold positions on pullback to $4500 as geopolitical hedge.
opportunities.rationale: Gold remains a safe haven; current weakness is temporary amid Iran tensions and potential Fed pivot.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in space stocks (Rocket Lab, AST SpaceMobile) on SpaceX IPO catalyst.
opportunities.rationale: SpaceX IPO euphoria lifts entire sector; Rocket Lab and AST have strong fundamentals and growth trajectories.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider crypto exposure via Strive or Bitcoin ETFs on Trump's supportive stance.
opportunities.rationale: Regulatory tailwinds and institutional adoption (Strive lifting holdings) support Bitcoin upside.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce discretionary and EM equity exposure; increase cash and short-duration bonds.
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical risks and oil spike threaten risk assets; cash provides optionality and bonds hedge recession risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge oil price risk via long energy stocks or oil futures.
defensive_moves.rationale: Iran strikes could sustain oil above $90; energy sector benefits from supply disruption and underinvestment.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid companies with governance scandals (BP, Starbucks) and monitor Synopsys settlement.
defensive_moves.rationale: Reputational damage and legal costs can depress stock prices; focus on companies with strong governance.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to China-linked commodities (coal, metals) due to disaster and demand slowdown.
defensive_moves.rationale: Chinese coal disaster disrupts supply chains; weak demand from China pressures base metals.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets will remain volatile due to conflicting US-Iran signals, oil price swings, and uncertainty around Fed/BOJ policy. Tech/AI may continue to rally on Nvidia catalyst, but consumer and energy headwinds could cap gains. S&P 500 near record highs but vulnerable to profit-taking.
market_outlook.long_term: AI-driven productivity gains and infrastructure buildout support structural bull case for tech. However, geopolitical fragmentation, inflation risks, and regulatory divergence (AI vs. crypto) create a bifurcated market. Energy transition and space exploration offer new growth avenues. Long-term investors should focus on quality, diversification, and thematic alignment.
key_catalysts: China stimulus and coal disaster fallout (headlines 24, 85)
Fed rate decision and inflation data (implied by oil/treasury moves)
watch_list: Oil price (WTI/Brent) and Iran strike updates
Nvidia stock price and AI conference news
Crypto regulation and prediction market developments
China industrial production and coal supply data
